utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi logan , how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hi . good to see you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's good to see you as well . so i know the nurse told you about dax . i'd like to tell dax a little bit about you . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so logan is a 57-year-old male , with a past medical history significant for coronary artery bypass grafting , hyperlipidemia , and ocd , who presents with an upper respiratory infection . so , logan , what's going on ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so , uh , about a week ago i started to get a cold . and , in the last two or three days , it seemed to move into my chest .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . um , and , and are you having any pain in your sinuses ? or , any post nasal drip ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , i had some post nasal drip for , again , for the beginning of the week . and then , uh , it , it left my , uh , my head . it went right down to my upper , uh , chest .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and have you gotten vaccinated against covid ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i actually have . i've , uh , gotten both vaccines .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: on both , both stages .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and anyone else sick as far as you know ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , not in my house . no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and have you had any fevers ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , i thought i had a slight fever , but no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , um , any shortness of breath ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , so let's just talk a little bit about your high cholesterol . how are you doing with the diet ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've been pretty good for the most part . uh , we did a little bit of travel over the last week or so , so the diet was n't great . but , for the most part it's been good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , i , i know we have you on the , on the crestor 20 milligrams a day . you're doing okay with that , so tolerating it ? no muscle aches , anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no muscle aches .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and i know you had the , uh , bypass grafting last year . you're doing okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's been great .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no chest pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: other than the congestion , no chest pain .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and you're able to exercise ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . before you got sick .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that's correct .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that's correct .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . well , let's go ahead , i want to do a quick physical exam . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you got it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , show me the vital signs . so , here it ... your vital signs look great . your oxygenation is 96 % . so that's very good . okay ? uh , i'm just going to check out your heart and lungs , and i'll let you know what i find . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so on exam , you do have some cervical lymphadenopathy . and your lungs have bilateral ronchi in them , um , and some wheezing . so , what that means is , you know , you have a little infection . you probably have a little bit of a cold , and you have some lymph nodes that are swollen , and some congestion in your lungs , like you said . let's go ahead and take a look at some of your results . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , show me the lipid panel . so your cholesterol , for the most part , is really good . i think you're doing a good job . uh , i do n't think you have to make any changes right now . um , hey dragon , show me the covid test . turns out you're positive for ... for covid .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so the results of your po- your covid test are positive .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , i know you were vaccinated . you probably have a , a , a very , um , low-level positivity . so let's talk about that . so for your first problem , uh , is your covid infection . and i think the vaccine has helped you . it seems to be very mild at this time . your oxygenation is really good . i want you to quarantine for t- for 10 days , so , uh , try to avoid your other household family members . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and you're going to have to take off work . um , if your symptoms worsen , i want you to call me .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's just supportive management . uh , for your next problem , your high cholesterol , let's continue you on the crestor 20 milligrams a day , and continue to watch your ja- your , your diet and i- then i'll go ahead and order another lipid panel for six months . and , from a coronary artery bypass grafting standpoint , your coronary artery disease seems under control . you're exercising . you do n't have any chest pain . we'll do a stress test in six months as well .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay ? any questions ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no questions .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . it was good to see you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: same here .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 57-year-old male with a history of coronary artery bypass grafting, hyperlipidemia, and OCD.
Upper respiratory infection
The patient reports cold symptoms starting one week ago, worsening over the last two to three days with chest congestion.
The doctor asks if the patient has sinus pain or postnasal drip.
The patient reports postnasal drip earlier in the week, which has since resolved in the head and moved to the chest.
The doctor asks if the patient has been vaccinated against COVID-19.
The patient confirms he has received both COVID-19 vaccine doses.
The doctor asks if anyone else in the household is sick.
The patient denies any household members are sick.
The doctor asks if the patient has had any fevers.
The patient denies having a fever.
The doctor asks if the patient has any shortness of breath.
The patient denies shortness of breath.
Hyperlipidemia
The doctor asks how the patient is doing with his diet.
The patient reports his diet is generally good, though it was poor during recent travel.
The patient is taking Crestor 20 mg daily.
The doctor asks if the patient is tolerating Crestor and experiencing muscle aches.
The patient denies muscle aches.
Coronary artery bypass grafting
The doctor asks if the patient has any chest pain.
The patient denies chest pain, noting only chest congestion.
The doctor asks if the patient is able to exercise.
The patient confirms he is able to exercise.
The doctor declares the intent to perform a physical exam.
The patient's oxygen saturation is 96%.
Physical exam reveals cervical lymphadenopathy, bilateral rhonchi, and wheezing.
The lipid panel is within normal limits.
The COVID-19 test result is positive.
The doctor advises the patient to quarantine for 10 days and avoid household family members.
The doctor instructs the patient to call if symptoms worsen.
The patient is to continue Crestor 20 mg daily.
The doctor will order a lipid panel in 6 months.
The doctor will order a stress test in 6 months.

case_description: upper respiratory infection; coronary artery bypass grafting;hyperlipidemia;ocd
